description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a distinctive red and white livery, featuring the word 'NORTHWEST' along the fuselage. The aircraft has a wide-body design with a prominent upper deck, four underwing turbofan engines, and a low-mounted swept wing configuration with slight upward-angled winglets at the tips. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is visible and consists of a tricycle arrangement with two main gear units and a nose gear. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew.
description: A large, red and white commercial passenger airplane with four underwing engines, swept low wings, and a conventional tail. The fuselage displays 'NORTHWEST' and features a forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, red upper fuselage, and white wings with red-tipped winglets.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the 'NORTHWEST' branding, four engines, and the full length of the fuselage and tail.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, engine arrangement, and the symmetry of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the red and white color scheme, engines, and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with upward-angled winglets at the tips and slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
